title: Protecting Bookstore Resources with Roles
statement: As a member of the bookstore's development team, you've been tasked with implementing role-based authorization for the bookstore's resources. Write an Express.js server that protects certain resources based on the user's role. You will be given a list of users and their roles. A GET request to `/books` should return a list of all books if the user has `admin` or `moderator` roles, while a GET request to `/books` should return only the books the moderator has access to if the user has only a `moderator` role. A GET request to `/books/1` should return the book with id 1 if the user has `admin`, `moderator`, or `author` roles. Users without any of these roles should receive a 403 Forbidden error.
constraints: Must use the provided users and roles data
Users and roles data should be stored in-memory

endpoints.description: Returns a list of books accessible to the user. The list should be filtered based on the user's role.
Returns the book with the specified id if the user has the correct role.
